risks: No managed identity configured — processor-app uses a connection string (COSMOS_DB_CONNECTION_STRING) to access Cosmos DB instead of managed identity with RBAC, violating least-privilege and credential-free best practices
No Key Vault resource for secrets management — sensitive values like connection strings are referenced inline rather than stored in Azure Key Vault
No VNet integration or private endpoints — all resources are publicly accessible, increasing attack surface for Cosmos DB, Container Registry, and the Container Apps environment
External ingress enabled on processor-app without network restrictions, exposing the processing endpoint to the public internet
PubSubConnector is not a recognized Azure resource type — it is a conceptual placeholder that will require mapping to an actual Azure service or external integration mechanism before deployment
No diagnostic settings configured for Event Grid topic, Cosmos DB account, or Container Registry — only Log Analytics workspace exists but is not wired to all resources
Single-region deployment with no redundancy, availability zones, or failover strategy (acknowledged assumption but still a reliability risk)
correctionsRecommended: Add a user-assigned managed identity resource and assign it to processor-app for credential-free access to both ACR (AcrPull role) and Cosmos DB (Cosmos DB Built-in Data Contributor role)
Replace COSMOS_DB_CONNECTION_STRING environment variable with managed identity-based RBAC authentication to Cosmos DB; disable key-based access on the Cosmos DB account
Add an Azure Key Vault resource to store any remaining secrets, and reference secrets from Key Vault in the Container App configuration
Add an explicit dependsOn entry from processor-app to cosmosdb — the app references Cosmos DB but does not declare this dependency
Add a dependsOn entry from pubsub-connector to ingest-topic — the connector description references ingest-topic but does not declare the dependency
Configure VNet integration for the Container Apps environment and use private endpoints for Cosmos DB and Container Registry
Add diagnostic settings resources to route Event Grid topic, Cosmos DB, and Container Registry logs to the Log Analytics workspace
Clarify or replace PubSubConnector with a concrete Azure resource (e.g., Event Grid partner topic, or document it as an out-of-band external integration)
